description:
The subsumption check in SpecRegistry::resolve slow path iterates the same
zipped key pairs twice per candidate:

  let all_le = ok.iter().zip(k.iter()).all(|(o, kk)| o.is_subtype(kk));
  let any_strict = ok.iter().zip(k.iter()).any(|(o, kk)| !kk.is_subtype(o));

Replace with a single fold that produces both booleans in one pass:

  let (all_le, any_strict) = ok.iter().zip(k.iter()).fold(
      (true, false),
      |(al, as_), (o, kk)| (al && o.is_subtype(kk), as_ || !kk.is_subtype(o)),
  );

This is a pure mechanical improvement — same result, half the iterations on
the key vectors. For narrow specs with long arities (closures with many
captured vars) the saving is proportional to arity.

Proven: existing SpecRegistry subsumption tests pass. All fixture tests pass.

## LANDING THE WORK
  Land this ticket before starting the next one:
  - Commit only the changes for this ticket, referencing fz-cg1.7
  - Close the ticket (`bw close fz-v9l.7`); It will tell you if work is newly unblocked
title: fz-cg1.7 subsumption single-pass fold in resolve slow path
